function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Following the xAI and X Holdings mergers, what related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure, and where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity?

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about related-party relationships within the consolidated structure following the xAI and X Holdings mergers, and where the filing discusses pre-merger intercompany activity. I need to look at the SpaceX S-1 filing.

Let me start by parsing the S-1 filing and then retrieving information about related-party transactions and intercompany activity.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

arguments.prompt: What related-party relationships exist within the consolidated structure following the xAI and X Holdings mergers? Where does the filing discuss pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings? Look for sections on related party transactions, common control, intercompany activity, and pre-merger transactions. {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
# Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

## I. Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

The filing addresses pre-merger intercompany activity between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings in several distinct locations:

### A. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section

This section (governed by Item 404 of Regulation S-K) explicitly extends to pre-merger activity:

> "the transactions described below also include certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of [the xAI Merger]."

It establishes a three-part threshold for disclosure: (1) the company is a participant; (2) the amount exceeds $120,000; and (3) a director, executive officer, 5%+ holder, or their immediate family member has a direct or indirect material interest.

### B. Note 1 — Nature of Business (Common Control Accounting)

The filing states that the Mergers were accounted for as **reorganizations of entities under common control** because Mr. Musk held a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X through his majority voting interest in each entity. Critically:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."

This means that pre-merger transactions among SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves are **not** separately disclosed as related-party transactions in the consolidated financial statements—they are eliminated. What remains disclosed are transactions with **external** related parties (Tesla, The Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) that persist post-merger.

### C. Prospectus Preamble (Retrospective Recasting)

> "The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI... and X Holdings... because these transactions were between entities under common control."

The preamble also notes this presentation "constitutes a change in reporting entity."

---

## II. Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

### A. Internal Corporate Structure (Post-Merger)

| Entity | Relationship | Acquisition Date |
|---|---|---|
| **SpaceX** (parent) | Reporting company | — |
| **xAI (X.AI Holdings Corp.)** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX | February 2, 2026 ("xAI Merger Date") |
| **X Holdings Corp. ("X")** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 ("X Merger") |
| **X.AI Corp.** | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 |
| **Falcon Landing, LLC** | SpaceX subsidiary operating aircraft | — |
| **CTC** | Subsidiary of xAI / indirect subsidiary of SpaceX | — |

All historical intercompany transactions among these entities have been eliminated in consolidation.

---

## III. External Related-Party Transactions (Ongoing Post-Merger)

### A. Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities

#### 1. Tesla, Inc.

Mr. Musk serves as CEO and director of Tesla and holds approximately 20% of Tesla stock. Tesla, in turn, holds 18,990,195 shares of SpaceX Class A common stock (<1.0% post-offering).

**SpaceX commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla:**

| Period | Goods/Services Obtained |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $11 million |
| 2024 | $4 million |
| 2025 | $144 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0.2 million |

**xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla:**

| Period | Goods/Services Obtained | Revenue Recognized by xAI |
|---|---|---|
| 2024 | $191 million | — |
| 2025 | $506 million | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $34 million | $0.4 million |

**X Holdings advertising agreements (Tesla purchasing ads on X):**

| Period | Advertising Purchased |
|---|---|
| 2024 | $0.5 million |
| 2025 | $4 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0 |

**Aircraft usage (SpaceX invoicing Tesla):**

Since April 2016, SpaceX has owned and operated aircraft used by Mr. Musk (in his capacity as Tesla CEO) and other Tesla personnel. Rates were determined by Tesla and SpaceX subject to FAA rules.

| Period | Amount Charged to Tesla |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $1 million |
| 2024 | $1 million |
| 2025 | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0 |

**Capital equipment purchases from Tesla:**

- Megapack products: $506 million (2025), $191 million (2024)—recorded in Property, plant, and equipment, net
- Cybertrucks: $131 million at MSRP (2025)—recorded in Property, plant, and equipment, net

#### 2. The Boring Company

- **Office lease:** In 2024, X entered into an office space lease with a Boring Company subsidiary. Lease payments: $0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan–Feb 2026).
- **Tunnel construction:** SpaceX incurred $1 million in 2025 for tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas.

#### 3. Musk Industries LLC (real property lease to xAI)

xAI leases real property owned by Musk Industries LLC (owned by Mr. Musk). Lease payments: $0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), $0.2M (Jan–Feb 2026).

#### 4. Security Company (Musk-owned)

SpaceX is party to a services agreement with a security company owned by Mr. Musk, organized to provide security services concerning him, including in connection with his SpaceX duties.

| Period | SpaceX Security Expenses |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $2 million |
| 2024 | $3 million |
| 2025 | $4 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $1 million |

#### 5. Elon Musk Stock Purchases

In 2025, Mr. Musk, through his trust, purchased $1,421 million of common stock from current and former shareholders.

---

### B. Transactions with Antonio J. Gracias and Valor Equity Partners

Mr. Gracias serves on SpaceX's board and is the founder, CEO, and Chief Investment Officer of Valor Equity Partners.

#### 1. Computing Equipment Leases (xAI subsidiaries → Valor)

Three equipment lease agreements between a subsidiary of xAI (CTC) and Valor for computing and related equipment:

| Lease | Aggregate Cash Payments (Life of Lease) |
|---|---|
| Lease 1 | $6,986 million |
| Lease 2 | $6,633 million |
| Lease 3 | $6,587 million |
| **Total** | **$20,206 million** |

- **Guarantor:** Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (or one of its subsidiaries) guarantees the lessees' payment and performance obligations.
- **Payments made:** $885 million in 2025; $857 million from January 1–February 28, 2026.

#### 2. Failed Sale-Leaseback Accounting (Valor Transaction)

The Valor transaction was deemed a **failed sale-leaseback**, resulting in:
- Debt recorded: $455 million (current) + $4,052 million (long-term) = **~$4,507 million** as of December 31, 2025 (one chunk references $5,365 million total, potentially including a second "Valor transaction II")
- Interest expense: $66 million for the year ended December 31, 2025
- Related asset recorded within Property, plant, and equipment, net

#### 3. X API Services (Valor → X)

X received payments from Valor for API services: $1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (Jan–Feb 2026).

---

### C. xAI Merger Consideration (Related-Party Transaction)

On February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued to xAI stockholders:
- **321.7 million shares** of Class A Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis)
- **121.7 million shares** of Class B Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis)
- **$2,947 million in cash**

---

### D. Tesla's xAI Investment and SpaceX Class A Issuance

- **January 2026:** Tesla agreed to invest $2,000 million in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, conditioned on regulatory approvals.
- **Following xAI Merger:** Tesla's right to acquire xAI Series E converted into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock.
- **March 12, 2026:** SpaceX issued 3.8 million shares of Class A Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis) to Tesla.

---

### E. Share Repurchases from Related Parties

| Period | Shares | Amount | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2024 | 38.7M SpaceX common | $920M | Eligible employees and existing shareholders |
| 2024 | 0.1M SpaceX preferred | $101M | Existing shareholders at fair market value |
| 2025 | 14.0M SpaceX common | $522M | Eligible current/former employees |
| 2025 | 11.8M xAI common | $600M | Existing xAI shareholder |
| Q1 2026 | 25.4M SpaceX common | $2,413M | Eligible current/former xAI employees (xAI Merger-related) |
| Q1 2026 | 30.5M SpaceX common + 2.1M preferred | $1,933M | Existing shareholders at fair market value |
| Q1 2025 | 28.0M SpaceX common | $508M | Eligible employees and existing shareholders |

All repurchased shares have been retired.

---

### F. Other Director and Executive Officer Transactions

SpaceX owns and operates, through Falcon Landing, LLC (a subsidiary), three aircraft for use by directors, executive officers, and other personnel.

---

## IV. Summary of Key Disclosure Locations

| Topic | Location in Filing |
|---|---|
| Common control accounting & elimination of intercompany transactions | Note 1 (Nature of Business); Prospectus preamble |
| Retrospective recasting of financial statements | Prospectus preamble (Selected Financial Data); Note 1 |
| Pre-merger agreements ongoing post-merger | "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section |
| Tesla commercial/advertising/aircraft transactions | "Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities" |
| Boring Company, Musk Industries, security company | Same section, subsequent paragraphs |
| Valor equipment leases and failed sale-leaseback | "Relationship with Antonio J. Gracias and Affiliated Entities"; Note 10 (Debt) cross-reference |
| xAI Merger consideration | Note 13 (Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity) |
| Tesla xAI investment conversion | Separate subsection within related party disclosures |
| Share repurchase activity | Capital stock roll-forward tables; Note 12/13 |

The filing's architecture is such that **intra-group** pre-merger activity (SpaceX ↔ xAI ↔ X) is eliminated and not separately disclosed, while **external** related-party transactions that continue post-merger are aggregated and presented by counterparty (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) with the understanding that some originated at the xAI or X level before consolidation.

arguments.prompt: Find the exact language in Note 1 (Nature of Business) about common control accounting, the elimination of intercompany transactions, and the retrospective recasting of financial statements to include xAI and X Holdings. Also find the exact language in the "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section about pre-merger agreements that continue post-merger. {{spacex_s1}}

results.content:
## Note 1 (Nature of Business) — Common Control Accounting, Elimination of Intercompany Transactions, and Retrospective Recasting

### Common-control reorganization and retrospective combination:

> "The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements. The Company's consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."

### Retrospective recasting to include xAI and X Holdings:

> "The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control, and (ii) a five-for-one stock split of the Company's Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock, effective May 4, 2026 (the '2026 Stock Split')."

---

## "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" — Pre-Merger Agreements Continuing Post-Merger

> "On February 2, 2026, we effected the xAI Merger, pursuant to which we acquired xAI (which includes X). For the purposes of the disclosures set forth in this section pursuant to Item 404 of Regulation S-K, the transactions described below also include certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of [the xAI Merger]."

2. Related-party relationships within the consolidated structure:
   - Internal corporate structure (SpaceX parent, xAI subsidiary, X Holdings subsidiary of xAI, etc.)
   - External related-party transactions (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities)

3. Key point: All historical intercompany transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X have been eliminated in consolidation because they were between entities under common control.

arguments.final_result:
## Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity Following the xAI and X Holdings Mergers

### 1. Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

The SpaceX S-1 addresses pre-merger intercompany activity in three principal locations:

**A. Note 1 — Nature of Business (Common-Control Accounting)**

The filing states that the Mergers were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control because Mr. Musk held a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X through his majority voting interest in each entity. Critically, Note 1 provides:

> "The Company's consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation**."

This means that pre-merger transactions *among SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves* are **not** separately disclosed as related-party transactions in the consolidated financial statements — they are eliminated. What remains disclosed are transactions with **external** related parties (Tesla, The Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) that persist post-merger.

**B. Prospectus Preamble (Retrospective Recasting)**

> "The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control..."

The preamble also notes this presentation "constitutes a change in reporting entity."

**C. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section (Item 404 of Regulation S-K)**

This section explicitly extends to pre-merger activity:

> "On February 2, 2026, we effected the xAI Merger, pursuant to which we acquired xAI (which includes X). For the purposes of the disclosures set forth in this section pursuant to Item 404 of Regulation S-K, the transactions described below also include certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of [the xAI Merger]."

It establishes a three-part threshold for disclosure: (1) the company is a participant; (2) the amount exceeds $120,000; and (3) a director, executive officer, 5%+ holder, or their immediate family member has a direct or indirect material interest.

---

### 2. Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

**A. Internal Corporate Structure (Post-Merger)**

| Entity | Relationship | Acquisition Date |
|---|---|---|
| SpaceX (parent) | Reporting company | — |
| xAI (X.AI Holdings Corp.) | Wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX | February 2, 2026 ("xAI Merger Date") |
| X Holdings Corp. ("X") | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 ("X Merger") |
| X.AI Corp. | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 |
| Falcon Landing, LLC | SpaceX subsidiary operating aircraft | — |
| CTC | Subsidiary of xAI / indirect subsidiary of SpaceX | — |

All historical intercompany transactions among these entities have been eliminated in consolidation.

**B. External Related-Party Transactions (Ongoing Post-Merger)**

The filing discloses ongoing related-party transactions with the following counterparties:

1. **Tesla, Inc.** — Mr. Musk serves as CEO and director of Tesla and holds ~20% of Tesla stock. Tesla, in turn, holds 18,990,195 shares of SpaceX Class A common stock (<1.0% post-offering). Disclosed transactions include:
   - SpaceX commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla ($11M in 2023; $4M in 2024; $144M in 2025; $0.2M Jan–Feb 2026)
   - xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla ($191M in 2024; $506M in 2025; $34M Jan–Feb 2026)
   - X Holdings advertising agreements (Tesla purchasing ads on X)
   - Aircraft usage (SpaceX invoicing Tesla since April 2016)
   - Capital equipment purchases from Tesla (Megapacks: $506M in 2025, $191M in 2024; Cybertrucks: $131M at MSRP in 2025)
   - Tesla's January 2026 agreement to invest $2,000M in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted post-merger into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock; SpaceX issued 3.8M shares to Tesla on March 12, 2026

2. **The Boring Company** — Office lease (X entered into an office space lease with a Boring Company subsidiary in 2024); tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas ($1M in 2025)

3. **Musk Industries LLC** — Real property lease to xAI ($0.5M in 2024; $2M in 2025; $0.2M Jan–Feb 2026)

4. **Security Company (Musk-owned)** — Services agreement for security services concerning Mr. Musk ($2M in 2023; $3M in 2024; $4M in 2025; $1M Jan–Feb 2026)

5. **Antonio J. Gracias / Valor Equity Partners** — Mr. Gracias serves on SpaceX's board and is the founder, CEO, and Chief Investment Officer of Valor Equity Partners. Disclosed transactions include:
   - Three equipment lease agreements between a subsidiary of xAI (CTC) and Valor for computing and related equipment, with aggregate cash payments of approximately $20,206 million over the life of the leases ($6,986M + $6,633M + $6,587M)
   - SpaceX (or one of its subsidiaries) guarantees the lessees' payment and performance obligations
   - Payments made: $885M in 2025; $857M from January 1–February 28, 2026
   - The Valor transaction was deemed a **failed sale-leaseback**, resulting in debt of approximately $4,507 million as of December 31, 2025, and interest expense of $66 million for the year ended December 31, 2025
   - X received payments from Valor for API services ($1M in 2024; $1M in 2025; $0.1M Jan–Feb 2026)

6. **Elon Musk Stock Purchases** — In 2025, Mr. Musk, through his trust, purchased $1,421 million of common stock from current and former shareholders

7. **xAI Merger Consideration** — On February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued to xAI stockholders 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock, 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis), and $2,947 million in cash

8. **Share Repurchases from Related Parties** — Various repurchases from eligible employees and existing shareholders, including Q1 2026 repurchases of 25.4M SpaceX common shares for $2,413M from eligible current/former xAI employees (xAI Merger-related)

9. **Falcon Landing, LLC** — SpaceX owns and operates, through this subsidiary, three aircraft for use by directors, executive officers, and other personnel

---

### 3. Key Analytical Takeaways

- **Intra-group pre-merger activity is eliminated, not disclosed**: Because the Mergers were accounted for as common-control reorganizations, all historical related-party transactions *among SpaceX, xAI, and X* have been eliminated in consolidation. The filing does not separately quantify these pre-merger intercompany flows.

- **External related-party transactions are aggregated by counterparty**: The "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section presents ongoing transactions by counterparty (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities), with the understanding that some originated at the xAI or X level before consolidation.

- **The disclosure architecture distinguishes between**: (i) eliminated intra-group transactions (Note 1); (ii) retrospectively recast historical financials (Prospectus preamble); and (iii) ongoing external related-party transactions that continue post-merger (Item 404 section).

- **Material ongoing exposure**: The Valor equipment leases (~$20.2 billion aggregate) and the failed sale-leaseback accounting treatment (~$4.5 billion debt) represent the most financially significant ongoing related-party relationship, with SpaceX guaranteeing the lessees' obligations.

---

### Summary of Key Disclosure Locations

| Topic | Location in Filing |
|---|---|
| Common control accounting & elimination of intercompany transactions | Note 1 (Nature of Business) |
| Retrospective recasting of financial statements | Prospectus preamble; Note 1 |
| Pre-merger agreements ongoing post-merger | "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section |
| Tesla commercial/advertising/aircraft transactions | "Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities" |
| Boring Company, Musk Industries, security company | Same section, subsequent paragraphs |
| Valor equipment leases and failed sale-leaseback | "Relationship with Antonio J. Gracias and Affiliated Entities"; Note 10 (Debt) cross-reference |
| xAI Merger consideration | Note 13 (Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity) |
| Tesla xAI investment conversion | Separate subsection within related party disclosures |
| Share repurchase activity | Capital stock roll-forward tables; Notes 12/13 |

    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm",
      "name": "SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement (filed May 20, 2026) — Note 1 (Nature of Business), Prospectus Preamble, and 'Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions' section"
    }

results.content:
## Related-Party Relationships and Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity Following the xAI and X Holdings Mergers

### 1. Where the Filing Discusses Pre-Merger Intercompany Activity

The SpaceX S-1 addresses pre-merger intercompany activity in three principal locations:

**A. Note 1 — Nature of Business (Common-Control Accounting)**

The filing states that the Mergers were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control because Mr. Musk held a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X through his majority voting interest in each entity. Critically, Note 1 provides:

> "The Company's consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation**."

This means that pre-merger transactions *among SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves* are **not** separately disclosed as related-party transactions in the consolidated financial statements — they are eliminated. What remains disclosed are transactions with **external** related parties (Tesla, The Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities) that persist post-merger.

**B. Prospectus Preamble (Retrospective Recasting)**

> "The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control..."

The preamble also notes this presentation "constitutes a change in reporting entity."

**C. "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" Section (Item 404 of Regulation S-K)**

This section explicitly extends to pre-merger activity:

> "On February 2, 2026, we effected the xAI Merger, pursuant to which we acquired xAI (which includes X). For the purposes of the disclosures set forth in this section pursuant to Item 404 of Regulation S-K, the transactions described below also include certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of [the xAI Merger]."

It establishes a three-part threshold for disclosure: (1) the company is a participant; (2) the amount exceeds $120,000; and (3) a director, executive officer, 5%+ holder, or their immediate family member has a direct or indirect material interest.

---

### 2. Related-Party Relationships Within the Consolidated Structure

**A. Internal Corporate Structure (Post-Merger)**

| Entity | Relationship | Acquisition Date |
|---|---|---|
| SpaceX (parent) | Reporting company | — |
| xAI (X.AI Holdings Corp.) | Wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX | February 2, 2026 ("xAI Merger Date") |
| X Holdings Corp. ("X") | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 ("X Merger") |
| X.AI Corp. | Wholly-owned subsidiary of xAI | March 28, 2025 |
| Falcon Landing, LLC | SpaceX subsidiary operating aircraft | — |
| CTC | Subsidiary of xAI / indirect subsidiary of SpaceX | — |

All historical intercompany transactions among these entities have been eliminated in consolidation.

**B. External Related-Party Transactions (Ongoing Post-Merger)**

The filing discloses ongoing related-party transactions with the following counterparties:

1. **Tesla, Inc.** — Mr. Musk serves as CEO and director of Tesla and holds ~20% of Tesla stock. Tesla, in turn, holds 18,990,195 shares of SpaceX Class A common stock (<1.0% post-offering). Disclosed transactions include:
   - SpaceX commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla ($11M in 2023; $4M in 2024; $144M in 2025; $0.2M Jan–Feb 2026)
   - xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla ($191M in 2024; $506M in 2025; $34M Jan–Feb 2026)
   - X Holdings advertising agreements (Tesla purchasing ads on X)
   - Aircraft usage (SpaceX invoicing Tesla since April 2016)
   - Capital equipment purchases from Tesla (Megapacks: $506M in 2025, $191M in 2024; Cybertrucks: $131M at MSRP in 2025)
   - Tesla's January 2026 agreement to invest $2,000M in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted post-merger into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock; SpaceX issued 3.8M shares to Tesla on March 12, 2026

2. **The Boring Company** — Office lease (X entered into an office space lease with a Boring Company subsidiary in 2024); tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas ($1M in 2025)

3. **Musk Industries LLC** — Real property lease to xAI ($0.5M in 2024; $2M in 2025; $0.2M Jan–Feb 2026)

4. **Security Company (Musk-owned)** — Services agreement for security services concerning Mr. Musk ($2M in 2023; $3M in 2024; $4M in 2025; $1M Jan–Feb 2026)

5. **Antonio J. Gracias / Valor Equity Partners** — Mr. Gracias serves on SpaceX's board and is the founder, CEO, and Chief Investment Officer of Valor Equity Partners. Disclosed transactions include:
   - Three equipment lease agreements between a subsidiary of xAI (CTC) and Valor for computing and related equipment, with aggregate cash payments of approximately $20,206 million over the life of the leases ($6,986M + $6,633M + $6,587M)
   - SpaceX (or one of its subsidiaries) guarantees the lessees' payment and performance obligations
   - Payments made: $885M in 2025; $857M from January 1–February 28, 2026
   - The Valor transaction was deemed a **failed sale-leaseback**, resulting in debt of approximately $4,507 million as of December 31, 2025, and interest expense of $66 million for the year ended December 31, 2025
   - X received payments from Valor for API services ($1M in 2024; $1M in 2025; $0.1M Jan–Feb 2026)

6. **Elon Musk Stock Purchases** — In 2025, Mr. Musk, through his trust, purchased $1,421 million of common stock from current and former shareholders

7. **xAI Merger Consideration** — On February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued to xAI stockholders 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock, 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock (pre-2026 Stock Split basis), and $2,947 million in cash

8. **Share Repurchases from Related Parties** — Various repurchases from eligible employees and existing shareholders, including Q1 2026 repurchases of 25.4M SpaceX common shares for $2,413M from eligible current/former xAI employees (xAI Merger-related)

9. **Falcon Landing, LLC** — SpaceX owns and operates, through this subsidiary, three aircraft for use by directors, executive officers, and other personnel

---

### 3. Key Analytical Takeaways

- **Intra-group pre-merger activity is eliminated, not disclosed**: Because the Mergers were accounted for as common-control reorganizations, all historical related-party transactions *among SpaceX, xAI, and X* have been eliminated in consolidation. The filing does not separately quantify these pre-merger intercompany flows.

- **External related-party transactions are aggregated by counterparty**: The "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section presents ongoing transactions by counterparty (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-affiliated entities), with the understanding that some originated at the xAI or X level before consolidation.

- **The disclosure architecture distinguishes between**: (i) eliminated intra-group transactions (Note 1); (ii) retrospectively recast historical financials (Prospectus preamble); and (iii) ongoing external related-party transactions that continue post-merger (Item 404 section).

- **Material ongoing exposure**: The Valor equipment leases (~$20.2 billion aggregate) and the failed sale-leaseback accounting treatment (~$4.5 billion debt) represent the most financially significant ongoing related-party relationship, with SpaceX guaranteeing the lessees' obligations.

---

### Summary of Key Disclosure Locations

| Topic | Location in Filing |
|---|---|
| Common control accounting & elimination of intercompany transactions | Note 1 (Nature of Business) |
| Retrospective recasting of financial statements | Prospectus preamble; Note 1 |
| Pre-merger agreements ongoing post-merger | "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section |
| Tesla commercial/advertising/aircraft transactions | "Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities" |
| Boring Company, Musk Industries, security company | Same section, subsequent paragraphs |
| Valor equipment leases and failed sale-leaseback | "Relationship with Antonio J. Gracias and Affiliated Entities"; Note 10 (Debt) cross-reference |
| xAI Merger consideration | Note 13 (Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity) |
| Tesla xAI investment conversion | Separate subsection within related party disclosures |
| Share repurchase activity | Capital stock roll-forward tables; Notes 12/13 |
